content:
Let 
\[
S \;=\; 9 + 9^2 + \cdots + 9^{2010}
\;=\;\frac{9^{2011}-9}{9-1}
\;=\;\frac{9(9^{2010}-1)}{8}.
\]
We check the smallest primes one by one:

1. \(p=2\):  each \(9^k\) is odd, so \(S\) is a sum of 2010 odd terms.  Since 2010 is even, \(S\equiv 0\pmod2\).  
2. \(p=3\):  \(9^k\) is divisible by 3, so \(S\equiv 0\pmod3\).  
3. \(p=5\):  \(9\equiv4\pmod5\), and \(4^1+4^2+4^3+4^4+\cdots\) has period 2 (sum \(=4+1=5\equiv0\pmod5\)); 2010 is even, so \(S\equiv0\pmod5\).  
4. \(p=7\):  \(9\equiv2\pmod7\), period 3, and \(2^1+2^2+2^3=7\equiv0\pmod7\); 2010 is a multiple of 3, so \(S\equiv0\pmod7\).  
5. \(p=11\):  \(9\) has order 5 mod 11, and \(9^1+\cdots+9^5\equiv0\pmod{11}\); 2010≡0 (mod 5), so \(S\equiv0\pmod{11}\).  
6. \(p=13\):  \(9\) has order 3 mod 13, and \(9^1+9^2+9^3\equiv0\pmod{13}\); 2010≡0 (mod 3), so \(S\equiv0\pmod{13}\).  
7. \(p=17\):  \(9\) has order 8 mod 17.  Writing \(2010=8\cdot251+2\), the full 8‐cycles each sum to 0 mod 17, but the remaining \(9^1+9^2\equiv9+13=22\equiv5\pmod{17}\).  Hence 
\[
S\not\equiv0\pmod{17}.
\]

Thus the smallest prime not dividing \(S\) is 

\boxed{17}.

message.content:
Let 
\[
S \;=\; 9 + 9^2 + \cdots + 9^{2010}
\;=\;\frac{9^{2011}-9}{9-1}
\;=\;\frac{9(9^{2010}-1)}{8}.
\]
We check the smallest primes one by one:

1. \(p=2\):  each \(9^k\) is odd, so \(S\) is a sum of 2010 odd terms.  Since 2010 is even, \(S\equiv 0\pmod2\).  
2. \(p=3\):  \(9^k\) is divisible by 3, so \(S\equiv 0\pmod3\).  
3. \(p=5\):  \(9\equiv4\pmod5\), and \(4^1+4^2+4^3+4^4+\cdots\) has period 2 (sum \(=4+1=5\equiv0\pmod5\)); 2010 is even, so \(S\equiv0\pmod5\).  
4. \(p=7\):  \(9\equiv2\pmod7\), period 3, and \(2^1+2^2+2^3=7\equiv0\pmod7\); 2010 is a multiple of 3, so \(S\equiv0\pmod7\).  
5. \(p=11\):  \(9\) has order 5 mod 11, and \(9^1+\cdots+9^5\equiv0\pmod{11}\); 2010≡0 (mod 5), so \(S\equiv0\pmod{11}\).  
6. \(p=13\):  \(9\) has order 3 mod 13, and \(9^1+9^2+9^3\equiv0\pmod{13}\); 2010≡0 (mod 3), so \(S\equiv0\pmod{13}\).  
7. \(p=17\):  \(9\) has order 8 mod 17.  Writing \(2010=8\cdot251+2\), the full 8‐cycles each sum to 0 mod 17, but the remaining \(9^1+9^2\equiv9+13=22\equiv5\pmod{17}\).  Hence 
\[
S\not\equiv0\pmod{17}.
\]

Thus the smallest prime not dividing \(S\) is 

\boxed{17}.
